subtitle: Antalya's long pebble city beach under the Taurus mountains and the travertine falez cliffs — a clear-watered Blue Flag shore that is also the front yard of one of the most-visited cities on the planet, on a coast founded 2,200 years ago as 'heaven on earth.'
spike_statement: The pebble city beach of Antalya under the Taurus mountains — the Blue-Flag shore of one of the most-visited cities on Earth, 17 million tourists a year, ahead of Paris.

levers.photo_tone: clear turquoise Mediterranean water on a pale-pebble shore, the Beydağları mountains rising straight from the sea behind the bay, travertine cliffs and Turkish pine, beach-park umbrellas stretching to the haze
